description.en: Retrieves the value set by set_escdelay(). Added in version 3.9.

description.en: Retrieves the value set by set_tabsize(). Added in version 3.9.

description.en: After getch() returns KEY_MOUSE to signal a mouse event, this method should be called to retrieve the queued mouse event, represented as a 5-tuple (id, x, y, z, bstate). id is an ID value used to distinguish multiple devices, and x, y, z are the event’s coordinates. (z is currently unused.) bstate is an integer value whose bits will be set to indicate the type of event, and will be the bitwise OR of one or more of the following constants, where n is the button number from 1 to 5: BUTTONn_PRES...

description.en: Return the current coordinates of the virtual screen cursor as a tuple (y, x). If leaveok is currently True, then return (-1, -1).

excerpt.es: Return the current coordinates of the virtual screen cursor as a tuple (y, x). If leaveok is currently True, then return (-1, -1).
excerpt.en: Return the current coordinates of the virtual screen cursor as a tuple (y, x). If leaveok is currently True, then return (-1, -1).

description.en: Read window related data stored in the file by an earlier window.putwin() call. The routine then creates and initializes a new window using that data, returning the new window object.

excerpt.es: Read window related data stored in the file by an earlier window.putwin() call. The routine then creates and initializes a new window using that data, returning the new window object.
excerpt.en: Read window related data stored in the file by an earlier window.putwin() call. The routine then creates and initializes a new window using that data, returning the new window object.

description.en: Return the window contents as a string; whether blanks in the window are included is affected by the stripspaces member.

excerpt.es: Return the window contents as a string; whether blanks in the window are included is affected by the stripspaces member.

description.en: Get a wide character. Return a character for most keys, or an integer for function keys, keypad keys, and other special keys. In no-delay mode, raise an exception if there is no input. Added in version 3.3.

description.en: Return the given window’s current background character/attribute pair.

excerpt.es: Return the given window’s current background character/attribute pair.
excerpt.en: Return the given window’s current background character/attribute pair.

description.en: Get a character. Note that the integer returned does not have to be in ASCII range: function keys, keypad keys and so on are represented by numbers higher than 255. In no-delay mode, return -1 if there is no input, otherwise wait until a key is pressed.

description.en: Get a character, returning a string instead of an integer, as getch() does. Function keys, keypad keys and other special keys return a multibyte string containing the key name. In no-delay mode, raise an exception if there is no input.

description.en: Return a tuple (y, x) of the height and width of the window.

excerpt.es: Return a tuple (y, x) of the height and width of the window.
excerpt.en: Return a tuple (y, x) of the height and width of the window.

description.en: Return the beginning coordinates of this window relative to its parent window as a tuple (y, x). Return (-1, -1) if this window has no parent.

excerpt.es: Return the beginning coordinates of this window relative to its parent window as a tuple (y, x). Return (-1, -1) if this window has no parent.
excerpt.en: Return the beginning coordinates of this window relative to its parent window as a tuple (y, x). Return (-1, -1) if this window has no parent.

description.en: Read a bytes object from the user, with primitive line editing capacity. The maximum value for n is 2047. Changed in version 3.14: The maximum value for n was increased from 1023 to 2047.

description.en: Return a tuple (y, x) of current cursor position relative to the window’s upper-left corner.
